link.description: Once a simulation runs, you want to watch it without rewriting your components. Akita's hooks are callbacks the framework invokes at defined points — before each event, when a port sends a message, or at points you define yourself. This section introduces the hook mechanism and how to expose your component's internal behavior, then covers tracing in depth: marking tasks, writing your own tracer, tracing the request lifecycle, the built-in tracers and their filters, and how the whole tracing system is built on hooks.
